key_metrics_output: Mass concentration (μg/ml) of polymeric component; polymer type identification and quantification (polyethylene terephthalate, polyethylene, polystyrene, poly(methyl methylacrylate)); detection via pyrolysis-GC/MS
abstract: This study developed a robust sampling and analytical method using pyrolysis double shot-gas chromatography/mass spectrometry to measure plastic particles ≥700 nm in human whole blood from 22 healthy volunteers. Four high production volume polymers were identified and quantified in blood for the first time: polyethylene terephthalate, polyethylene, polymers of styrene, and poly(methyl methylacrylate). The mean sum quantifiable concentration of plastic particles in blood was 1.6 μg/ml, demonstrating that plastic particles are bioavailable for uptake into the human bloodstream and establishing the first measurement of mass concentration of polymeric components in human blood.

particle_size_range: particles ≥700 nm in human whole blood
operationally defined method targeted particles that could be retained on a filter with pore size of 700 nm, i.e. particles ≥700 nm in dimension
key_metrics_output: Four high production volume polymers applied in plastic were identified and quantified
Polyethylene terephthalate, polyethylene and polymers of styrene...were the most widely encountered, followed by poly(methyl methylacrylate)

pyrolysis double shot - gas chromatography/mass spectrometry
abstract: This study's goal was to develop a robust and sensitive sampling and analytical method with pyrolysis double shot - gas chromatography/mass spectrometry and apply it to measure plastic particles ≥ 700 nm in human whole blood
Four high production volume polymers applied in plastic were identified and quantified for the first time in blood
the mean of the sum quantifiable concentration of plastic particles in blood was 1.6 µg/ml
plastic particles are bioavailable for uptake into the human bloodstream
